part1Topics.required: Can you describe your typical daily routine?
follow_ups: What time do you usually wake up in the morning?
How do you usually spend your evenings after work or study?
Has your daily routine changed compared to a few years ago?
Do you prefer following a strict schedule or being more flexible with your time?

part1Topics.required: Do you enjoy cooking? Why or why not?
follow_ups: How often do you eat out at restaurants or order food in?
What is your favourite meal of the day and why?
Are eating habits in your country changing? In what way?
Do you prefer home-cooked food or restaurant food and why?

part1Topics.required: How important is technology in your daily life?
follow_ups: How much time do you spend on your phone each day?
Which apps or websites do you use most often and why?
Do you think people rely too much on technology in their daily lives?
How has technology changed the way you communicate with friends and family?

part1Topics.required: What do you enjoy doing in your free time?
follow_ups: Have your hobbies changed since you were a child?
Do you prefer spending your free time indoors or outdoors? Why?
How much free time do you have each week?
Do you think it is important to have hobbies? Why?

part2.title: Describe a change you made to your daily routine that had a positive effect on your life.
prompts: What the change was
When and why you made this change
What difficulties you faced in making the change
Why the change has had a positive effect

questions: Why do you think many people struggle to maintain a healthy daily routine?
How has remote working changed the way people structure their day?
Do you think modern lifestyles are becoming too busy? Why?
Should employers do more to support a healthy work-life balance for their staff?

part2.title: Describe a memorable meal you have had.
prompts: Where you had the meal and who you were with
What you ate and what made it special
How you felt during and after the meal
Why this meal was so memorable

questions: How has globalisation changed the food culture in your country?
Do you think people today are more health-conscious about food than in the past?
What are the advantages and disadvantages of fast food for modern society?
Should governments do more to regulate unhealthy food products? Why?

part2.title: Describe a piece of technology that has had a significant impact on your life.
prompts: What the technology is
When you started using it and how you use it
What you did before you had access to this technology
Why it has had such a significant impact on your life

questions: How has technology transformed the way people work over the last decade?
Do you think social media has had a positive or negative effect on society overall?
How might artificial intelligence change daily life in the next twenty years?
Should children be given smartphones? At what age is it appropriate?

part1Topics.required: Are you currently studying or working? Tell me about it.
follow_ups: Did you enjoy school when you were younger?
What subjects did you find most interesting at school and why?
Do you think it is important to continue learning after formal education ends?
How do you prefer to study — alone or with others?

part1Topics.required: How concerned are you personally about environmental issues?
follow_ups: Do you do anything in your daily life to protect the environment?
Are environmental issues discussed a lot in your country?
Have you noticed any changes to the environment where you live?
Do you think individuals can make a real difference to environmental problems?

part1Topics.required: How important is keeping healthy to you in your daily life?
follow_ups: Do you do any regular exercise? What kind?
Do you think you have a healthy diet? Why or why not?
How do you cope with stress in your life?
Has your attitude to health changed as you have got older?

part1Topics.required: What kind of work would you like to do in the future, or what do you do now?
follow_ups: What is the most important thing to you in a job — salary, satisfaction, or something else?
Would you prefer to work for a company or be self-employed? Why?
Do you think it is important to have a good relationship with your colleagues?
How do you feel about working overtime or taking work home?

part2.title: Describe a teacher or lecturer who had a great influence on you.
prompts: Who this person was and what subject they taught
How long you knew this person
What made this teacher different from others
How this teacher influenced your life or studies

questions: How important is a university degree for finding a good job today?
In what ways has technology changed the experience of studying at school or university?
Do you think private schools provide a better education than state schools? Why?
What skills do you think future generations will need that are not currently taught in schools?

part2.title: Describe a natural place you have visited that made a strong impression on you.
prompts: Where this place is and how you got there
What you saw and did there
Who you went with or whether you went alone
Why this place made such a strong impression on you

questions: Who bears the most responsibility for protecting the environment — governments, businesses, or individuals?
How effective do you think international agreements on climate change are?
Do you think economic development and environmental protection can go hand in hand?
What lifestyle changes do you think people need to make to tackle climate change?

part2.title: Describe a time when you successfully maintained a healthy habit for a period of time.
prompts: What the habit was
How long you maintained it and how you started
What challenges you faced in keeping up the habit
What benefits you noticed from maintaining this habit

questions: To what extent should governments be responsible for the health of their citizens?
Why do you think mental health has become such an important topic in recent years?
How has modern technology affected people's physical health — positively and negatively?
Do you think people today are more or less healthy than people were fifty years ago? Why?
